methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $93M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Cognichip, a Series A chip design startup founded by Faraj Aalaei, is evaluated against a peer set of 4 private semiconductor companies at the same stage (Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI) alongside 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two valuation methods triangulate to a range. The Stage-Progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the company's $93M total raised, yielding $186M. The Peer-Median method anchors to the median valuation of four same-stage, same-sector peers at $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-Comp Anchoring is not applicable; while the public semiconductor sector trades at a median P/S of 20.6x across the three tickers, Cognichip's revenue is not disclosed and cannot support a revenue multiple analysis.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $158M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $843M. The wide dispersion reflects significant uncertainty around Cognichip's relative competitive position and growth trajectory within a heterogeneous peer set. The Stage-Progression floor of $186M assumes capital deployment as a valuation proxy; the Peer-Median ceiling of $1.5B assumes Cognichip's technology and market opportunity align with top-quartile comparables. Key uncertainties include product-market fit validation, capital efficiency relative to peers, and whether recent semiconductor capital formation reflects sustainable valuations or transient market conditions.
